item.title: Ukrainian, Russian strikes kill 13 civilians
item.description: A Ukrainian drone strike on a bus in Russia’s Belgorod region killed five people including a child Monday, authorities said, while Kyiv said Russian strikes on several Ukrainian cities left at least eight dead....

item.title: ACC inventories Ziaul Ahsan’s Jalshiri house
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission on Monday began an inventory of valuables at a house owned by Maj Gen (relieved) Ziaul Ahsan in the Jalshiri residential area of Rupganj, Narayanganj, as part of an ongoing corruption investigation....

item.title: MALAYSIA SYNDICATE FRAUD: Govt revokes licences of 49 recruiting agencies
item.description: The government has revoked the licences of 49 recruiting agencies following a case filed over allegations of sending workers to Malaysia through a syndicate while charging excessive recruitment fees....

item.title: DHAKA-ASHULIA EXPRESSWAY: Cost may go up by 55pc, time by 4 years
item.description: The construction cost of the Dhaka-Ashulia Elevated Expressway, now being built, is likely to go up by around 55 per cent to Tk 27,046 crore due to the proposed addition of three intersections to the largely Chinese-funded project, officials said....

item.title: BMU adopts OBC to elevate education standards
item.description: A compilation of 38 outcome-based curricula across 31 departments was presented to vice-chancellor Professor Dr FM Siddiqui during the 75th academic council meeting of Bangladesh Medical University at its Shaheed Dr Milton Hall on Sunday......Humanrights articles

item.title: BRAC Univ welcomes 3 new BoT members
item.description: BRAC University has appointed three new members to its board of trustees......

item.title: ONE YEAR OF MILESTONE JET CRASH: No compensation paid, no case filed till now
item.description: A year after a Bangladesh Air Force fighter jet crashed into Milestone School and College in Uttara, killing 38 people – most of them children – and injuring 63, the victims and their families are yet to receive any compensation from the government....

item.title: PM’s defence adviser calls for boosting cyber capabilities, indigenous innovation
item.description: Prime minister’s defence adviser retired brigadier general AKM Shamsul Islam visited the Military Institute of Science and Technology at Mirpur Cantonment in Dhaka on Monday......

item.title: GP auditor flags Tk 125.80b BTRC tax demand as ‘unresolved risk’
item.description: The auditor of Grameenphone Ltd has issued an emphasis of matter in its report on the telecom operator’s interim financial statements for the six months ended June 30, 2026, drawing attention to an unresolved Tk 125.80 billion demand notice from the Bangladesh Telecommunication Regulatory Commission, disclosure documents filed with the Dhaka Stock Exchange showed on Monday......

item.title: CMU holds discussion on July uprising
item.description: Chittagong Medical University on Monday held a discussion and award ceremony to honour the martyrs and injured in the July uprising......ExecutiveBranch

item.title: ULAB explores strategic partnership with Huawei
item.description: The University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh held a level meeting with a delegation from Huawei, exploring partnership opportunities between ULAB and Huawei Bangladesh, at the university in Dhaka on Sunday......

item.title: Floods cause Tk 402cr agri losses
item.description: With a stretch of Bangladesh spanning its north and northeast, along with parts of central Bangladesh, facing a fresh flood threat over the next three days, a government estimate has put the agricultural losses from the flash floods that hit in the second week of July at Tk 402 crore....

item.title: Two trust funds established at Dhaka University
item.description: Two new trust funds, the Dr Mazid Scholarship Trust Fund and the Rosie Anwara Mazid Trust Award, have been established at Dhaka University to support student scholarships and research initiatives......

item.title: Reducing NPLs requires more than directives
item.description: IT IS welcome that the BangladeshBankhas issued specific instructions to 29banksstruggling with high levels of non-performing loans. Among these 29 banks, a few are burdened with NPL ratios exceeding 80–90 per cent. A number of state-owned banks are also grappling with defaulted loans ranging from 20 to 60 per cent. In separate meetings with the managing......GeographicReference

item.title: Govt should rein in unhealthy food marketing
item.description: THE unaffordability of healthy food and the unregulated marketing of unhealthy food have affected people’s food consumption behaviour in ways that could further contribute to the burden of non-communicable diseases. A study on rural food environment and the dietary changes in South Asia, published in the British Journal of Nutrition, analysed data from nearly......

item.title: Not to be hurried, not to be tarried
item.description: A PIECE of news flashed online on July 2 — in online editions of some print newspapers and on some news portals. It has said that the merger of eight University of Dhaka departments into three has been discussed. The deans’ committee of the university, at its meeting on June 22, has discussed whether three departments under the social sciences faculty could be......

item.title: Rape victims’ exposure rampant despite ban
item.description: Two weeks after the brutal rape and murder of an eight-year-old girl at Dhaka’s Pallabi, the victim’s father appeared at a roundtable to raise a serious question over exposing the victim and her family....

item.title: Climate crisis is an economic crisis
item.description: FOR years, climate change was viewed primarily as an environmental concern. In Bangladesh, that distinction has disappeared. Intensifying cyclones, prolonged floods, riverbank erosion, saline intrusion, heatwaves and erratic rainfall are no longer simply altering the country’s physical landscape; they are reshaping its economy. Climate resilience is therefore no longer just.....Humanrights articles

item.title: Never again, only some lives matter
item.description: MORE than 30 years after the Srebrenica massacre, Bosnia remains deeply divided. Officially one state, it is effectively two — the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina, mainly Bosniak and Croat, and Republika Srpska, predominantly Serb. The Dayton accords have institutionalised this division, creating a separation akin to ethnic apartheid. In Serb-run areas, schools......

item.title: The republic of leaks
item.description: I GREW up in a small thana sohor, where everyone knew everyone else’s business long before the official record did. A government officer bought a new motorcycle, and within an hour the bazaar had already decided whether he had stolen the money. A school teacher received a promotion and someone inevitably whispered that he must have ‘managed’ it. We learned......

item.title: ‘The pain is immense,’ says Messi of World Cup defeat
item.description: Lionel Messi said on Monday that after Argentina's World Cup final loss ‘the pain is immense and it's going to take a long time for this wound to heal.’...News

item.title: World Cup champs Spain lap up adulation of a million on bus parade
item.description: New World Cup champions Spain began their celebration parade on an open-top bus through the streets of Madrid on Monday early evening, with a jubilant crowd of over a million people out to celebrate, local authorities told AFP....

item.title: Colonel Taher killing day today
item.description: The 50th anniversary of the execution of Colonel Abu Taher Bir Uttam will be observed today....News

item.title: Parents abandon free govt schools for costly non-govt options
item.description: Many parents in Rajshahi division are increasingly turning away from free government primary schools and enrolling their children in fee-charging kindergartens and other non-government institutions....

item.title: July martyrs’ families renew call for justice
item.description: Families of those killed and injured during the July uprising on Monday renewed their demands for justice, implementation of the July Charter, rehabilitation of injured protesters and proper recognition of the sacrifices made during the mass movement....
